articles.title: Scientists say this type of olive oil could boost brain power
articles.description: Extra virgin olive oil might help protect your brain by working through your gut. A two-year study found that people who consumed it had better cognitive performance and more diverse gut bacteria than those using refined olive oil. Researchers even identified specific microbes linked to these benefits. The findings suggest that choosing high-quality olive oil could be a simple way to support brain health as you age.

articles.title: Pinpointing barriers to timely head and neck cancer treatment in rural areas
articles.description: A new study led by researchers at Dartmouth Cancer Center pinpoints why many patients in rural areas experience delays in receiving critical follow-up treatment for head and neck cancer, and what can be done to address those gaps. Published in JAMA Otolaryngology–Head & Neck Surgery, the study examines factors that influence whether patients begin postoperative radiotherapy within the recommended six-week window after surgery, the timeframe known to improve survival for patients with head and neck squamous cell carcinoma.

articles.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed in response to U.S. Navy blockade
articles.description: In a major reversal, Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, less than 24 hours after it announced the critical waterway was “completely open.” It comes as Iran allegedly opened fire on two ships attempting to pass through the strait. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said the closure is a response to the continuing U.S. Navy blockade on Iranian ports. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez has the latest.

articles.title: Experimental treatment for pancreatic cancer could be breakthrough for patients

articles.title: The RAM shortage could last years
articles.description:
According to Nikkei Asia, even as suppliers ramp up DRAM production, manufacturers are only expected to meet 60 percent of demand by the end of 2027. SK Group chairman has even said that shortages could last until 2030.
The world's largest memory makers - Samsung, SK Hynix, and Micron - are all working to add new fabrication capacity, but almost none of it will be online until at least 2027, if not 2028. SK opened a fab in Cheongju in February, but that is the only increase in production among the three for 2026.
Nikkei says that production would need to increase by 12 percent a year in 2026 and 2027 to meet demand. But according to Counte …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: 3D-printed brain sensors may unlock personalized neural monitoring
articles.description: Soft electrodes designed to perfectly match a person's brain surface may help advance neural interfaces for neurodegenerative disease monitoring and treatment, according to a new study led by Penn State researchers. Neural interfaces are powered by tiny sensors capable of tracking biophysical signals, known as bioelectrodes. These sensors are usually made from stiff materials in a one-size-fits-all design that struggles to match the brain's complex structure. The researchers have created a novel approach to 3D printing bioelectrodes that can stretch and morph to fit the minor differences that make every brain unique.

articles.title: A mass shooting in Ukraine's capital leaves 6 dead before police shot and killed the gunman
articles.description: A gunman has killed six people and injured at least 14 in a mass shooting in Ukraine's capital before he was shot and killed by police.

articles.title: Osteopenia: Loss of bone mineral density affects millions of people. Here's what you need to know
articles.description: Around 40% of adults worldwide are affected by osteopenia: a loss of bone mineral density. This condition is extremely common, particularly in postmenopausal women and elderly adults. It's estimated that more than 500,000 fractures occur annually in the UK due to low bone density.

articles.description: Conway wrote that he “will be stepping back from some of my usual activities,” but he will “continue to support” founders backed by his firm SV Angel.

articles.title: TCS Nashik case update: ‘Matter is sensitive, revealing,’ says fact-finding committee member amid ongoing probe
articles.description: A fact-finding committee is investigating allegations of religious conversion and sexual harassment at Tata Consultancy Services' Nashik office. A panel member called the matter ‘sensitive’ and ‘revealing.’

articles.title: Bihar CM slams opposition over women's quota bill
articles.description: Samrat Choudhary said it was a "black day" when the Constitution amendment bill fell in Lok Sabha and "women's reservation was mocked by parties like Congress and TMC"

articles.title: 'Cowardly, hypocritical': Mamata Banerjee rejects PM Modi’s charge on Women’s Reservation Bill
articles.description: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee accused PM Modi of misleading the nation regarding the women's reservation bill, alleging the Centre is using the exercise as a shield for delimitation. She questioned the timing of the bill's push, especially with state elections approaching, and criticized the Prime Minister's public address on the matter.

articles.title: What the ‘science’ of delimitation and fertility struggles to capture
articles.description: Readers familiar with the politics of delimitation will find that the scholarly literature catches up to their intuitions only slowly and incompletely. There are good reasons why

topStories.title: Bihar CM slams opposition over women's quota bill
topStories.description: Samrat Choudhary said it was a "black day" when the Constitution amendment bill fell in Lok Sabha and "women's reservation was mocked by parties like Congress and TMC"

topStories.title: ‘Mentioned Congress 59 times, women barely’: Kharge leads opposition charge on PM Modi's speech
topStories.description: Opposition leaders Mallikarjun Kharge, Mamata Banerjee, and D.K. Shivakumar have strongly criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi, accusing him of misrepresenting facts regarding the women's reservation bill. They contend the government used the bill as a political shield for delimitation, not genuine empowerment, and that the bill's passage was deliberately stalled. Leaders emphasized Congress's long-standing support for women's representation.

topStories.title: Police investigating potential shooting near University of Iowa, campus says
topStories.description: "First responders on scene. Confirmed victims," the university said in an alert.

topStories.title: Just Like That: Why it is hard to trump a civilisational State
topStories.description: Iran conflict highlights civilisational vs nation-state strength, with Iran’s cultural depth shaping resilience beyond military power.

topStories.title: Titanic survivor's life jacket sells for over $900K at auction, far exceeding price expectations
topStories.description: A life jacket worn by Titanic survivor Laura Mabel Francatelli sold for roughly $906,000 at auction, far exceeding its pre-sale estimate of $475,000.

topStories.title: ‘TMC didn't want more daughters as MLAs and MPs’: PM Modi in Bengal after women quota bill failure
topStories.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi accused the Trinamool Congress of opposing the women's reservation bill, claiming the party worked against greater political representation for Bengal's daughters. He asserted the BJP prioritizes women's empowerment and safety, promising improved welfare delivery if elected in the state.

topStories.title: Buoyed by Brunson, Knicks seal 'hard-fought win'
topStories.description: Jalen Brunson crossed the 20-point mark for the 38th time in a Knicks uniform, finishing with 28 on Saturday at Madison Square Garden, and New York posted a 113-102 victory over the Atlanta Hawks in Game 1 of their Eastern Conference first-round series.

topStories.title: Jade Colvin is Missing
topStories.description: Photos discovered on an old cellphone help solve the disappearance of a 14-year-old girl. "48 Hours" correspondent Natalie Morales reports.

topStories.title: Kennard, James team to upset Rockets in opener
topStories.description: Luke Kennard scored a career playoff-high 27 points, LeBron James had 19 points and 13 assists, and the short-handed Los Angeles Lakers capitalized on Kevin Durant's injury absence for a 107-98 victory over the Houston Rockets in Game 1.

topStories.title: Iran live updates: Tehran reviewing US proposal as it reasserts control over strait
topStories.description: President Donald Trump announced "major combat operations" against Iran on Feb. 28, with massive joint U.S.-Israeli strikes.

topStories.title: Iran fires on ships in Strait of Hormuz as it closes the critical chokepoint again
topStories.description: Iran swiftly reversed course on reopening the Strait of Hormuz after the U.S. said the move would not end its blockade.

topStories.description: True crime. Real justice. To miss it would be a crime.

dailyNews.description: Former President Barack Obama met privately with New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani before joining together to read to preschoolers at a child care center in the Bronx.

dailyNews.title: Bron Breakker crushes Seth Rollins with two spears in epic return at WrestleMania 42
dailyNews.description: Bron Breakker blindsided Seth Rollins with a vicious spear at WrestleMania 42, allowing Gunther to lock in a sleeper hold and force a tap out.

dailyNews.title: Investigators team up to solve mystery of missing Iowa teen
dailyNews.description: After Jade Colvin, a missing Iowa teenager, is tracked to a remote farm, photos discovered on an old cellphone reveal important clues.

dailyNews.title: Cody Rhodes retains Undisputed WWE Championship at WrestleMania 42, but Randy Orton gets last licks
dailyNews.description: Cody Rhodes defeated Randy Orton to retain the Undisputed WWE Championship at WrestleMania 42 in Las Vegas after Pat McAfee's wild interference at the event.

dailyNews.title: Nicole Kidman recalls being alone in Venice the night she learned her mother had died
dailyNews.description: Nicole Kidman opens up about her mother's death, her harrowing night alone in Venice and her newfound plans to become a death doula after loss.

dailyNews.title: Elon Musk fulfills teen's last wish in touching X post after she was too weak to take his call
dailyNews.description: A 15-year-old cancer patient dreamed of meeting Elon Musk. After she died, he answered her handwritten questions and made her plushie a SpaceX mascot.

dailyNews.title: Paige makes triumphant WWE return at WrestleMania 42, captures tag titles with Brie Bella
dailyNews.description: Paige stunned the WrestleMania 42 crowd with a surprise return, defeating Alexa Bliss with the RamPaige to become a women's tag team champion.

dailyNews.title: Potential 2028 Democrats Audition in Michigan,With a Focus on Trump
dailyNews.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris, Senator Cory Booker of New Jersey and Gov. Andy Beshear of Kentucky spoke at a gathering of party insiders in Detroit, fueling presidential speculation.

dailyNews.title: Skydiver rescued after crashing into scoreboard during Virginia Tech game
dailyNews.description: A skydiver crashed into the scoreboard before Virginia Tech's spring football game.

dailyNews.title: US Coast Guard spots overturned vessel near Saipan during search for missing ship
dailyNews.description: The Mariana is a 145-foot dry cargo vessel registered in the U.S. It suffered engine failure Wednesday as a massive typhoon bore down on Saipan and nearby islands.

dailyNews.title: U.S. Installs a Trump Loyalist to Lead ‘Grand Conspiracy’ Case Into Trump Foes
dailyNews.description: A former lawyer for President Trump’s campaign, Joseph diGenova, is said to be planning to split time between Miami and Fort Pierce, where a grand jury overseen by a Trump-favored judge sits.

dailyNews.title: Iran declares Strait of Hormuz closed in response to U.S. Navy blockade
dailyNews.description: In a major reversal, Iran has declared the Strait of Hormuz closed, less than 24 hours after it announced the critical waterway was “completely open.” It comes as Iran allegedly opened fire on two ships attempting to pass through the strait. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard said the closure is a response to the continuing U.S. Navy blockade on Iranian ports. NBC News’ Raf Sanchez has the latest.

topStories.description: The president is directing federal agencies to boost research into the drugs and support clinical trials, moves championed by Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.

topStories.title: Trump signs executive order accelerating research into psychedelic drug therapies
topStories.description: President Donald Trump signed an executive Saturday that aims to accelerate research and approval of psychedelic-based therapies for conditions such as PTSD.

topStories.title: Trump Signs Executive Order to Loosen Restrictions on Psychedelic Drugs
topStories.description: The president’s executive order is intended to accelerate research into the compounds’ efficacy in treating mental health disorders like severe depression and PTSD.

topStories.title: U.S. Officials Visited Havana to Lay Out Proposals for Cuban Reforms
topStories.description: The delegation told Cuba’s leadership that it had only a narrow window of time to make the economic and political changes demanded by the Trump administration.

topStories.title: How the dispute between Trump and Pope Leo escalated
topStories.description: President Trump has lobbed insults at Pope Leo XIV in response to his criticisms of the war in Iran, marking an unusually pronounced rupture between the leaders of the world's most powerful country and the world's largest Christian denomination.

topStories.title: Pope Leo says he is not trying to debate Trump, but preach peace in Africa
topStories.description: Pope Leo pushed back on claims that he is feuding with President Trump. Chris Livesay has more details.

topStories.title: Americans see some relief at the pump
topStories.description: President Trump accused Iran of blackmailing the U.S. on Saturday, but there is some relief for Americans. Gas prices fell, averaging just over $4 a gallon nationwide. Olivia Rinaldi reports from the White House on the war's ripple effects.

dailyNews.description: Watch live coverage as White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt holds a press briefing.

dailyNews.description: The president has made immigration an early priority to start second term, with ICE making more than 900 arrests Sunday.

dailyNews.description: The president, calling the heavily bombed enclave “a demolition site,” proposed relocating Palestinians temporarily or long-term, a move rejected by Arab countries since the war began.

dailyNews.title: Beijing Signals Readiness to Talk to Trump's Team, Even Old Foes
dailyNews.description: The Chinese foreign minister spoke by phone with Marco Rubio, the new secretary of state who has been a critic of Beijing.

dailyNews.title: Canada Is Becoming a Fentanyl Exporter, and a Target for Trump
dailyNews.description: The drug is increasingly manufactured in and trafficked from the U.S.’s northern neighbor, though the numbers remain small compared with Mexico.

dailyNews.title: We analyzed Trump’s public remarks. His White House ballroom is a top priority.
dailyNews.description: The president has publicly highlighted his White House ballroom project on roughly a third of the days this year, a Washington Post analysis found.

dailyNews.title: In Angola, love for an American pope but not for an American president
dailyNews.description: In Luanda, Catholics expressed love and admiration for Pope Leo XIV, the first American pope, but not so much for President Trump because of the war in Iran.
